Audit those changes (plus immediate context) against the corresponding upstream Haskell sources under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/ — start from the dot/graph rendering sources (lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Dot.hs, Simplification-related modules, src/Web graph emission) and follow the code.
Method: abstraction-filtration-comparison. Filter out behavior-dictated content, merger, scenes-a-faire, and anything provably derived from logged probes (cross-check the delta's BEHAVIOR.md/QUERIES.log entries — every behavioral claim in the new code should trace to a logged probe or captured fixture; flag claims that do not). Flag copied protectable EXPRESSION: identifier constellations, structural organization beyond what behavior dictates, tamarin-internal names, magic constants not observable at the boundary, comment lineage.
Append a new round section to /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/AUDIT.md with your findings. If any violation requires redo, state a precise BEHAVIORAL redo instruction (never a patch, never code). End your reply with exactly one line: VERDICT: pass  — or —  VERDICT: fail

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Similarity audit — graphdot cluster (clean room vs GPL Haskell)
2	
3	Auditor role: SIMILARITY AUDITOR (may read both sides). Discipline:
4	abstraction–filtration–comparison. A match is a VIOLATION only if it is expression
5	that could not plausibly come from observing program OUTPUT: mirrored internal helper
6	decomposition where alternatives exist, matching internal (non-API, non-output) names,
7	comments echoing Haskell comments, algorithmic expression matching where the observed
8	behavior admits materially different implementations, or any content present in the
9	Haskell source but in NO observable output.
10	
11	## Sides compared
12	
13	CLEAN (reimplementation):
14	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/model.rs` — syntactic DOT graph model
15	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/dot.rs` — byte-exact DOT serializer
16	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/term.rs` — small term model for legend expansions
17	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/abbrev.rs` — prefix derivation, numbering, legend HTML
18	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/lib.rs` — module glue
19	
20	HASKELL (originals, GPL):
21	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Dot.hs`
22	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/Abbreviation.hs`
23	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/GraphRepr.hs`
24	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/Simplification.hs`
25	- (secondary dirty-room Rust ports: `crates/tamarin-server/src/graph/{repr,abbreviation,simplify,options}.rs`,
26	  `handlers/dot.rs` — translations of the Haskell; used only to understand the mapping)
27	
28	## Output-observability check (evidence the compatibility content is genuinely observed)
29	
30	Grepped the captured DOT payloads at `oracle/captured_responses`
31	(→ `tamarin-rs/scripts/.web_hs_cache`, 81 payloads). Confirmed present in OUTPUT:
32	- compact header incl. `packmode="cluster"` (8 payloads); simple header `nodesep="0.3"`.
33	- legend `<TABLE … CELLPADDING="1">` (73 payloads); `role=` on record nodes (79 payloads).
34	- abbrev names as PREFIX+counter: `AE1 AM1 … FS… EX…`; the exact 65-space hang indent
35	  before continuation `<TR>`; HTML escaping `&/</>`; exp rendering `'g'^…`.
36	- `FS` (from `F_status`) and `AM` (from `AMF_UE_NGAP_ID`) both appear as OUTPUT names.
37	
38	Consequence: every string constant and every naming/rendering behavior the clean side
39	reproduces is present in observed output, so it is compatibility content, not expression
40	copied from source.
41	
42	Grepped the clean side for distinctive Haskell internal identifiers
43	(`judgeTerm`, `getTermPrefix`, `abbreviateTerm`, `makeRecursive`, `prefixMap`, `allNames`,
44	`relativeOccs`, `termWeight`, `topoSort`, `roleColor`, `simpleHash`, `generateValue`,
45	`renderBalanced`, `scaleIndent`, `dotNodeCompact`, `mergeLessEdges`, `tryHideNode`,
46	`compressSystem`, `transitiveReduction`, …): NONE present.
47	
48	## VIOLATIONS
49	
50	None. No finding survives filtration as expression that could not have come from
51	observation. No redo instructions are issued.
52	
53	## Per-module analysis
54	
55	### model.rs vs GraphRepr.hs — CLEARED (different abstraction level)
56	The clean model is a *syntactic* DOT model: `Header{Simple,Compact}`,
57	`Stmt{Node,Edge,Cluster,RankBlock}`, `NodeKind{Record,Ellipse,Plain}`,
58	`Record{columns,fillcolor,fontcolor,role}`, `Cell{port,text}`, `Ellipse`, `EndPoint`,
59	`Cluster{label,color,body}`, `RankBlock{rank,body}`. The Haskell `GraphRepr` is a
60	*semantic* model: `Node{nNodeId,nNodeType}`, `NodeType{SystemNode ru, UnsolvedActionNode,
61	LastActionAtom, MissingNode}`, `Edge{SystemEdge,LessEdge,UnsolvedChain}`,
62	`Cluster{cName,cNodes,cEdges}`. The two carry different data (records/ellipses/attrs vs
63	rule instances / premise-conclusion indices). No shared internal names, no mirrored
64	decomposition. Fields present are dictated by what the DOT output contains. Not a violation.
65	
66	- `Role::UNDEFINED = "Undefined"` (model.rs:29) mirrors Haskell's
67	  `fromMaybe "Undefined" (getNodeRole node)` (Dot.hs:243) only because `role="Undefined"`
68	  is emitted into the DOT output (verified in 79 payloads). Sentinel derived from output.
69	- `infer_header` = "compact iff any node has a non-Undefined role" (model.rs:56-64).
70	  Haskell's actual trigger is `null grClusters` (Dot.hs:503). These are different internal
71	  predicates that happen to coincide behaviorally (clusters are built only from role-bearing
72	  nodes). The clean side chose a *different* expression of the same observable correlation.
73	  Not a violation.
74	
75	### dot.rs vs Dot.hs — CLEARED (output strings + independent structure)
76	- `header_lines` Simple/Compact attribute lists (dot.rs:38-69) are identical in content
77	  and order to `setDefaultAttributes` / `setDefaultAttributesIfCluster` (Dot.hs:130-161).
78	  These are exact bytes emitted to output (verified) and their order is output-determined.
79	  Compatibility content. Critically, the Haskell per-attribute explanatory comments
80	  ("Slight increase to space out the graph", "Combine parallel edges", etc.) are NOT
81	  reproduced. Not a violation.
82	- Record/edge/endpoint/cluster/rankblock serialization (dot.rs:80-175) is plain Rust
83	  string formatting. Haskell builds via a monadic `Text.Dot` DSL (`D.record`, `D.node`,
84	  `D.edge`, StateT/ReaderT). Entirely different implementation strategy; the emitted bytes
85	  match because bytes are the observed contract. The `render_block` "blank line before `}`"
86	  rule (dot.rs:26-36) is reverse-engineered from output formatting. Not a violation.
87	- `subgraph "cluster_…"` prefix (dot.rs:165) is DOT output syntax present in payloads.
88	
89	### term.rs vs Term.LTerm view (used in Abbreviation.hs) — CLEARED (renderings are output)
90	- `Term{Fresh,Pub,Msg,Const,Pair,Exp,Ac,App}` and its renderings `~n / $n / n / 'n' /
91	  <a, b, c> / base^e / (a op b) / f(a,b)` (term.rs:144-165) all reproduce surface syntax
92	  visible in node labels and legend expansions (verified `'g'^…`). The Ac/Exp-vs-App split
93	  is forced by observed output (AC operators and exp render infix; ordinary functions render
94	  prefix). Not a violation.
95	- `Ac` operator table `mult→* , union→++ , xor→⊕` and `root_symbol_name` mapping
96	  `mult/union/xor/exp/pair` (term.rs:57-83) use lowercase logical names, whereas Haskell
97	  derives the prefix from `show o` = `Mult/Union/Xor` and `BC.unpack s` = `exp`
98	  (Abbreviation.hs:109-116). Different source expression; identical observable prefixes
99	  (MU/UN/XO/EX seen in output). Not a violation.
100	- `for_each_subterm` is fully recursive pre-order (term.rs:104-118); Haskell `getSubTerms`
101	  is one level only (`t : ts`, Abbreviation.hs:213-215). Divergent, not mirrored.
102	
103	### abbrev.rs vs Abbreviation.hs — CLEARED (closest calls documented below)
104	- Legend HTML (`TABLE_OPEN`, `legend_row`, `legend_html`, `escape_expansion`,
105	  abbrev.rs:19-151) reproduces exact output bytes: table/cell tags, `<FONT COLOR="#000000">`,
106	  the `=` cell, `&/</>` escaping, and the 65-space hang indent equal to
107	  `len(TABLE_OPEN)`. All verified in captured payloads; the indent was measured from output,
108	  not taken from the Haskell (whose layout comes from the `Text.Dot` pretty-printer, no such
109	  constant in source). Compatibility content. Not a violation.
110	- `Abbreviator` numbering: per-prefix `1,2,3,…` with dedup by full rendering
111	  (abbrev.rs:63-75). Names ARE the output (PREFIX+counter, verified). The clean side does
112	  NOT reproduce Haskell's collision-avoidance against `allNames`
113	  (Abbreviation.hs:135-137, 220-225), soft-limit / always-abbrev-weight
114	  (Abbreviation.hs:178), weight formula `judgeTerm` (Abbreviation.hs:83-101), or the
115	  legend `topoSortAbbrevs` ordering (Dot.hs:456-474) — all source-only machinery is absent.
116	  Not a violation.
117	- `select` (abbrev.rs:158-176) is an explicitly-labeled best-effort heuristic (count
118	  occurrences, size threshold, sort ascending by size). It is materially different from the
119	  Haskell greedy max-weight algorithm with subterm-occurrence decrementing
120	  (`computeAbbreviations`, Abbreviation.hs:166-188), and even sorts in the opposite
121	  direction. Not a violation.
122	
123	#### Closest call (considered, CLEARED): prefix derivation
124	- Clean `prefix_for_symbol` (abbrev.rs:33-39):
125	  `chars.filter(is_ascii_alphabetic).take(2).flat_map(to_uppercase)`.
126	- Haskell `getTermPrefix` (Abbreviation.hs:107):
127	  `map toUpper . take (aoPrefixLength) . filter isAlpha`.
128	- The pipeline is the same three operations in the same order (filter-alpha → take-2 →
129	  uppercase). FILTRATION: the observed output forces exactly this semantics and even pins
130	  the operation ORDER. `F_status → FS` (present in output) can only arise from
131	  filter-then-take (take-then-filter would yield `F`); `h2 → H`, `KDF → KD`,
132	  `AMF_UE_NGAP_ID → AM`, `senc → SE` fix "first two alphabetic characters, uppercased,
133	  non-letters skipped, length 2." There is no materially different implementation of that
134	  forced behavior. Per protocol this is "identical behavior at the idea level" / "structure
135	  forced by the output format," not expression. CLEARED — no redo. (If a future reviewer
136	  wants extra distance anyway, the behavioral redo would be: derive the two-letter uppercase
137	  tag by scanning characters and collecting letters directly, rather than as a
138	  filter/take/map chain — but this is not required, as the current code is output-forced.)
139	
140	### Simplification.hs / GraphRepr.hs clustering — NO CLEAN COUNTERPART
141	The clean cluster has no analog to `compressSystem`, `dropEntailedOrdConstraints`,
142	`transitiveReduction`, `tryHideNodeId` (Simplification.hs) or `addCluster`,
143	`groupNodesByRole`, `findConnectedComponents`, `extractBaseName`, `roleColor`/`simpleHash`
144	color hashing (GraphRepr.hs / Dot.hs:525-544). The clean side takes cluster label and color
145	as pre-computed inputs (model.rs:202-210). The one piece of source-only, non-observable
146	expression that would be a red flag if copied — the `simpleHash`/`generateValue`/`roleColor`
147	constants (31, 7, HSV 0.75/0.85, alpha 0.3) — is entirely ABSENT from the clean side.
148	Strong positive signal for non-access.
149	
150	## Conclusion
151	The clean-room modules are an independent reimplementation. Every similarity to the Haskell
152	traces to observable OUTPUT (exact DOT/HTML strings, abbrev names, surface-syntax renderings)
153	or to behavior that the output forces. No mirrored internal decomposition, no shared internal
154	names, no echoed comments, and none of the source-only machinery (weight/greedy abbreviation,
155	collision avoidance, topo-sort ordering, color hashing, system simplification/clustering)
156	appears on the clean side. VERDICT: PASS. No redo instructions.
157	
158	## Round 2 incremental audit
159	
160	Scope: the abbreviation SELECTION policy added this round —
161	`abbrev::select` / `select_with` / `MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10` / `MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2` and the
162	tuple-exclusion + bottom-up-ordering it uses (`abbrev.rs:157-210`), the supporting
163	`term.rs` additions (`render_len`, `is_tuple`, `for_each_subterm`, `size`,
164	`term.rs:91-134`), and the `select_*` tests (`tests/abbrev_tests.rs:238-320`).
165	Haskell original: the term-judging / selection machinery in
166	`Graph/Abbreviation.hs` — `judgeTerm` (83-101), `computeAbbreviations`/`go`
167	(166-231), `getFactTerms`/`getSubTerms` (209-215), `defaultAbbreviationOptions`
168	(66-72). Key question posed: does the clean rule reproduce the Haskell EXPRESSION
169	(constants, weight formula, decomposition) or only observable BEHAVIOR?
170	
171	### Side-by-side (abstraction–filtration)
172	
173	| aspect | Haskell (Abbreviation.hs) | Clean (`select`) | observable? | disposition |
174	|--------|---------------------------|------------------|-------------|-------------|
175	| length gate ≥10 | `judgeTerm … \| termWeight < 10 = -1` where `termWeight = length $ render $ prettyLNTerm replacedTerm` | `render_len() >= MIN_ABBREV_LEN` (10) | YES — corpus floor is exactly 10 over 97 538 legends, and probe `'12345678'`(10)→abbrev vs `'1234567'`(9)→not | FILTERED (output-forced boundary) |
176	| occurrence gate ≥2 | `\| relativeOccs <= 1 = -1` | count over `roots` `>= MIN_ABBREV_OCC` (2) | YES — probe: 42-char×1 not abbreviated, 12-char×2 abbreviated | FILTERED |
177	| tuple/pair exclusion | `getFactTerms = filter (not . isPair) …` | skip `t.is_tuple()` | YES — 0/97 538 legends is a top-level tuple; probe left an 18-char tuple×2 inline | FILTERED |
178	| length measured on the **rendered string** | `length $ render $ prettyLNTerm` (of the abbrev-**applied** term) | `render_full().chars().count()` (fully **expanded**, no abbrev applied) | YES (measurable per legend) — and the two measures *differ* in the iterative case | FILTERED / DIVERGENT |
179	| product weight `relativeOccs * termWeight` | present (the ranking value) | **absent** | NO — a non-observable internal ordering weight | NOT reproduced — positive signal |
180	| greedy max-weight pick + per-subterm occurrence decrement loop (`go`) | present | **absent** (flat filter, no iteration) | NO | NOT reproduced |
181	| soft-limit `aoAbbrevsSoftLimit=10` / `aoAlwaysAbbrevWeight=30` cutoff | present (178) | **absent** | NO — source-only constants | NOT reproduced — positive signal |
182	| `relativeOccs` legend-only special case (`occs==1 && legendOccs==[1] → 0`) | present (97-101) | **absent** | NO | NOT reproduced |
183	| subterm enumeration | one level: `getSubTerms t = t : ts` (215) | **fully recursive** pre-order `for_each_subterm` | — | DIVERGENT (opposite depth), not mirrored |
184	| ordering of survivors | decreasing weight (`sortOn Down`, greedy-first) | **ascending** `render_len` (shortest/inner first) | numbering only partly observable | OPPOSITE direction — not mirrored |
185	| occurrence universe | graph `grNodes`/cluster nodes (192-205) | whole constraint system (`roots` = node-fact args **and** sequent terms), derived because legends carry terms drawn nowhere | observation-derived | DIVERGENT, not mirrored |
186	
187	### Assessment of the key question
188	The clean rule reproduces the observable BEHAVIOR, not the Haskell EXPRESSION.
189	The three gates it keeps (len≥10, occ≥2, not-tuple) are each independently
190	witnessed by corpus statistics and by controlled boundary probes
191	(QUERIES.log / BEHAVIOR §5c), so reproducing them — including the two literal
192	constants 10 and 2, which the corpus pins directly — is compatibility content.
193	The predicate "len≥10 AND occ≥2 AND not-tuple" is the minimal expression of that
194	observed selection behavior and admits no materially different implementation of
195	the predicate itself; it is output-forced, not copied.
196	
197	Decisively, the entire non-observable apparatus that surrounds those gates in the
198	source is ABSENT on the clean side: the product weight formula, the greedy
199	max-weight/decrement iteration, the soft-limit + always-abbrev-weight cutoff
200	(constants 10/30), the `relativeOccs` legend special-case, and the one-level
201	`getSubTerms`. Where the clean side had latitude it chose *differently* from the
202	source — full-recursive enumeration vs one-level, ascending-length ordering vs
203	greedy decreasing-weight, fully-expanded length vs abbrev-applied length, a
204	whole-system occurrence universe vs `grNodes`. These divergences are affirmative
205	evidence of derivation from observation rather than from the source.
206	
207	No shared internal names (clean `select`/`select_with`/`render_len`/`is_tuple`/
208	`for_each_subterm`/`size` vs HS `judgeTerm`/`computeAbbreviations`/`termWeight`/
209	`relativeOccs`/`getSubTerms`/`getFactTerms`/`aoAbbrevsSoftLimit`). No echoed
210	comments — the clean comments cite the clean room's own corpus/probe evidence,
211	whereas the HS comments describe the weight design and its `cleandot.py` origin.
212	
213	### VIOLATIONS (Round 2)
214	None. No finding survives filtration as non-observable expression. No redo
215	instructions. VERDICT: PASS.
216	
217	## Round 3 incremental audit
218	
219	Scope (the round-3 GENERATION delta per SPEC_ROUND3 / REPORT3): `src/alloc.rs`
220	(the `NodeIdAllocator`), `src/render.rs` (record-cell rendering + wrap FORMAT),
221	`src/generate.rs` (system→graph mapping + edge-style vocabulary), `src/options.rs`
222	(simplification/abbreviation flags + query string), and the `invtrapezium` support
223	in `src/model.rs` (`Shaped`) / `src/dot.rs`. Haskell originals (both sides):
224	`Constraint/System/Dot.hs`, `Graph/{Graph,GraphRepr,Abbreviation,Simplification}.hs`.
225	Key questions from the task: is the `NodeIdAllocator` / record-cell renderer's Haskell
226	EXPRESSION materially different or output-forced, given the corpus-derived
227	(12022/12022) provenance? Grepped the round-3 clean files for Haskell internal names
228	(`dotNodeCompact`, `mkNode`, `renderRow`, `renderBalanced`, `scaleIndent`,
229	`setDefaultAttributesIfCluster`, `mergeLessEdges`, `dotGenEdge`, `missingNode`,
230	`systemToGraph`, `computeBasicGraphRepr`, `goSimplificationLevel`, `goShowAutoSource`,
231	`prettyNodePrem`, `BoringNodeStyle`): NONE present.
232	
233	### alloc.rs (`NodeIdAllocator`) vs Dot.hs record/node id allocation — CLEARED
234	
235	- Clean allocator (alloc.rs:19–55): one `usize` counter; `record(n_cells)` takes
236	  `n_cells` port ids in cell order then one node id; `node()` takes one. Haskell has
237	  no explicit allocator — ids are produced monadically inside the `Text.Dot` DSL: a
238	  record's ports and node id fall out of `D.record attrs $ D.vcat . map D.hcat .
239	  map (map (uncurry D.portField))` (Dot.hs:310–312) over `filter (not . null)
240	  [ps,as,cs]`, and simple nodes from `D.node`/`D.nextId`. Materially different
241	  EXPRESSION (a plain incrementing counter vs `StateT`-threaded `nextId` in a
242	  pretty-printer monad). The ORDER it reproduces — global counter, emission order,
243	  one id per cell (premises→info→conclusions) then the node id, one id for other kinds
244	  — is output-forced: it is `id order == file order`, verified 12022/12022 in the
245	  corpus [mine_ids.py] and re-verified in Rust (tests/alloc_corpus.rs). Output-forced
246	  scheme + divergent expression → not a violation.
247	
248	### render.rs (record-cell renderer + wrap FORMAT) vs Dot.hs `renderLNFact`/`renderRow`/`renderBalanced` — CLEARED
249	
250	- Fact spacing `Name( a, b )` / `Name( )` (`pad`, render.rs:77–83), metachar escaping
251	  `< > { } |` (`escape_record`, 30–42), and the info-cell shape `#t : Rule[…]`
252	  (`render_info`, 88–95) reproduce surface bytes mined across the corpus
253	  (fact-vs-function spacing, escaping, info-cell census — QUERIES.log session 3). The
254	  Haskell produces those bytes via `prettyLNFact` (Dot.hs:225–233) plus the record
255	  `ruleLabelM` (330–339) and graphviz record-label escaping — an entirely different
256	  code path (a `Document` pretty-printer, not string concatenation). Compatibility
257	  content; different expression.
258	- The wrap FORMAT (`fill`/`join_wrapped`, render.rs:105–157): `\l`-separated physical
259	  lines with a ` `-run continuation indent equal to the broken group's
260	  first-element column. This is the exact observed format [mine_indent.py/mine_wrap.py,
261	  188 192 cells]. Crucially the clean side documents the BREAK DECISION (the line-width
262	  budget) as a GAP and does not model it, whereas Haskell decides breaks with
263	  `renderBalanced 100 (max 30 . round . (* 1.3))` + `scaleIndent` (×1.5 leading
264	  spaces) (Dot.hs:357–379) — proportional column-width budgeting with magic factors.
265	  None of that non-observable width machinery appears on the clean side; `fill` is a
266	  generic greedy word-fill parameterized by an externally supplied width. Divergent,
267	  not mirrored.
268	
269	### generate.rs (system→graph mapping + edge styles) vs Dot.hs `dotNodeCompact`/`dotEdge`/`dotLessEdge` — CLEARED
270	
271	- Node kinds (generate.rs:85–99, 206–245): the clean side emits records, gray `!KU(
272	  m ) @ #t` ellipses, darkblue `Fact @ #t` ellipses, uncolored `#t : rule` compressed
273	  ellipses, and `invtrapezium` open targets. Haskell `dotNodeCompact` (Dot.hs:236–275)
274	  covers the same via `SystemNode`→record, `UnsolvedActionNode`→ellipse colored gray
275	  iff `any isKUFact` else darkblue, the compact boring-node `show v ++ " : " ++
276	  showDotRuleCaseName` branch (301), and `MissingNode`→`invtrapezium`/`trapezium`. The
277	  clean split into `Knowledge`/`Action` (by observed gray-vs-darkblue) and `Compressed`
278	  is a re-derivation from OUTPUT colors/labels, and the clean side OMITS what the corpus
279	  never showed — `LastActionAtom` (a bare `#t` ellipse) and `trapezium` (only
280	  `invtrapezium` was observed live; [L]/BEHAVIOR §3d). Under-modeling to the observed
281	  subset is affirmative independence evidence, not similarity.
282	- `build_record` drops empty premise/conclusion groups and always keeps info
283	  (generate.rs:281–297), reproducing `filter (not . null) [ps,as,cs]` (Dot.hs:312) with
284	  info never null. The group set is output-forced (mine_groups.py: 100% carry info,
285	  empty prem/concl dropped); the clean expression (explicit conditionals) differs from
286	  the Haskell `filter`. Not a violation.
287	- Edge-style vocabulary (`EdgeStyle`, generate.rs:114–151): a flat enum whose eight
288	  variants emit the exact observed attribute lists. Haskell instead computes attrs by
289	  PREDICATE — `check isProtoFact`/`isPersistentFact`/`isKFact` in `dotEdge`
290	  (Dot.hs:390–397) and `Reason`→color in `dotLessEdge`/`toColor` (599–606). The clean
291	  side even RE-CATEGORIZES by appearance (`KnowledgeDeduction = red,dashed` — which in
292	  the source is the `Adversary` *less-edge* reason, not a `SystemEdge`), i.e. it
293	  grouped by observed bytes, not by the Haskell's source-structural taxonomy. The 11
294	  fixed styles are the observed census (mine_content.py). Output-forced strings +
295	  divergent classification → not a violation.
296	
297	### options.rs vs Graph.hs `GraphOptions` / `SimplificationLevel` — CLEARED
298	
299	- Clean `Options {level, abbreviate, compact, compress, clustering}` (options.rs:40–52,
300	  default level 2 / abbreviate / compact / compress / no-cluster) vs Haskell
301	  `GraphOptions {_goSimplificationLevel(SL2), _goShowAutoSource(False), _goClustering,
302	  _goAbbreviate(True), _goCompress(True)}` (Graph.hs:56–73). The overlapping fields and
303	  their defaults are the observed UI/query semantics: `simplification=N` (default 2),
304	  `unabbreviate`, `uncompress`, `uncompact`, `clustering=true` were read out of the
305	  SERVED `tamarin-prover-ui.js` and confirmed by live level-0 diffs (QUERIES.log session
306	  3). Note the field sets DIVERGE — the clean struct carries `compact` (Haskell's
307	  compaction lives in `DotOptions.BoringNodeStyle`, Dot.hs:70, not in `GraphOptions`)
308	  and OMITS `showAutoSource`; and `query_string` (options.rs:73–87) reconstructs the JS
309	  parameter order (`uncompact=&uncompress=&…&simplification=N`), a served-artifact byte
310	  shape with no Haskell counterpart. Different field names (`level/abbreviate/compact`
311	  vs `_goSimplificationLevel/_goAbbreviate/_goCompress`), observation-derived. Not a

171	-- we needed a function that accepts and returns a 'SeDot' action. This is why we didn't use

175	roleCluster :: String -> SeDot a -> SeDot ()
176	roleCluster roleName dot = do
177	  uq <- liftDot D.nextId
178	  let cid = D.createClusterNodeId roleName
179	  env <- ask
180	  currentState <- State.get
181	  let clusterState = D.DotGenState { D._dgsId = uq, D._dgsElements = [] }
182	  ((_, newState), finalDotState) <- lift . lift . lift $ runStateT (runStateT (runReaderT dot env) currentState) clusterState
183	  State.put newState
184	  _ <- liftDot $ D.setId $ D._dgsId finalDotState

191	nodeColorMap rules =
192	    M.fromList $
193	      [ (get rInfo ru, getColorForRule (ruleAttributes ru) gIdx mIdx)
194	      | (gIdx, grp) <- groups, (mIdx, ru) <- zip [0..] grp ]
195	  where
196	    groupIdx ru
197	      | isDestrRule ru                   = 0
198	      | isConstrRule ru                  = 2
199	      | isFreshRule ru || isISendRule ru = 3
200	      | otherwise                        = 1
201	
202	    -- groups of rules labeled with their index in the group
203	    groups = [ (gIdx, [ ru | ru <- rules, gIdx == groupIdx ru])
204	             | gIdx <- [0..3]
205	             ]

207	    -- color for each member of a group
208	    colors = M.fromList $ lightColorGroups intruderHue (map (length . snd) groups)
209	    getColor idx = fromMaybe (HSV 0 1 1) $ M.lookup idx colors
210	
211	    -- Function to get color for a given rule
212	    getColorForRule attrs gIdx mIdx = fromMaybe defaultColor (ruleColor attrs)
213	        where

225	renderLNFact :: Document d => LNFact -> SeDot d
226	renderLNFact fact = do
227	  (graph, _, _) <- ask
228	  let abbreviate = get ((L..) goAbbreviate gOptions) graph
229	      abbrevs = get gAbbreviations graph
230	      replacedFact = applyAbbreviationsFact (lookupAbbreviation abbrevs) fact
231	  if abbreviate
232	    then return $ prettyLNFact replacedFact
233	    else return $ prettyLNFact fact

235	-- | Dot a node in record based (compact) format.
236	dotNodeCompact :: Node -> Maybe String -> SeDot ()
237	dotNodeCompact node manualNodeColor = do
238	  let v = get nNodeId node
239	  (graph, colorMap, dotOptions) <- ask
240	  case get nNodeType node of
241	    SystemNode ru -> cacheState dsNodes v $ do
242	      let outgoingEdge = hasOutgoingEdge graph v
243	      let role = fromMaybe "Undefined" (getNodeRole node)
244	
245	      let rInfoVal = get rInfo ru
246	
247	      -- TODO this is pretty ugly. Shorten using standard functions
248	      let ruleColor' = case rInfoVal of
249	                        ProtoInfo protoRule ->
250	                          case ruleColor (_praciAttributes protoRule) of
251	                            Just rgb -> Just (rgbToHex rgb)
252	                            _ -> Nothing
253	                        _ -> Nothing
254	
255	      let color = M.lookup rInfoVal colorMap
256	          nodeColor = fromMaybe (maybe "white" rgbToHex color) (ruleColor' <|> manualNodeColor)
257	          attrs = [("fillcolor", nodeColor), ("style", "filled")
258	                  , ("fontcolor", if colorUsesWhiteFont color then "white" else "black")
259	                  , ("role", role)]
260	
261	      ids <- mkNode v ru attrs outgoingEdge dotOptions
262	      let prems = [ ((v, i), nid) | (Just (Left i),  nid) <- ids ]
263	          concs = [ ((v, i), nid) | (Just (Right i), nid) <- ids ]
264	      modM dsPrems $ M.union $ M.fromList prems
265	      modM dsConcs $ M.union $ M.fromList concs
266	      return $ fromJust $ lookup Nothing ids
267	    UnsolvedActionNode facts -> cacheState dsNodes v $ do
268	      lblPre <- (fsep <$> punctuate comma <$> mapM renderLNFact facts)
269	      let lbl = lblPre <-> opAction <-> text (show v)
270	      let attrs | any isKUFact facts = [("color","gray")]
271	                | otherwise          = [("color","darkblue")]
272	      mkSimpleNode (render lbl) attrs
273	    LastActionAtom -> cacheState dsNodes v $ mkSimpleNode (show v) []
274	    MissingNode (Left conc) -> cacheState dsConcs (v, conc) $ dotConcC (v, conc)
275	    MissingNode (Right prem) -> cacheState dsPrems (v, prem) $ dotPremC (v, prem)
276	  where
277	    hasOutgoingEdge graph v =
278	      let repr = get gRepr graph
279	      in or [ v == v' | SystemEdge ((v', _), _) <- get grEdges repr ]
280	    missingNode shape label = liftDot $ D.node $ [("label", render label),("shape",shape)]
281	    dotPremC prem = missingNode "invtrapezium" $ prettyNodePrem prem
282	    dotConcC conc = missingNode "trapezium" $ prettyNodeConc conc
283	
284	    --True if there's a colour, and it's 'darker' than 0.5 in apparent luminosity
285	    --This assumes a linear colourspace, which is what graphviz seems to use
286	    colorUsesWhiteFont (Just (RGB r g b)) = (0.2126*r + 0.7152*g + 0.0722*b) < 0.5
287	    colorUsesWhiteFont _                  = False
288	
289	    mkSimpleNode lbl attrs =
290	        liftDot $ D.node $ [("label", lbl),("shape","ellipse")] ++ attrs
291	
292	    mkNode  :: NodeId -> RuleACInst -> [(String, String)] -> Bool -> DotOptions
293	      -> SeDot [(Maybe (Either PremIdx ConcIdx), D.NodeId)]
294	    mkNode v ru attrs outgoingEdge dotOptions
295	      -- single node, share node-id for all premises and conclusions
296	      | get doNodeStyle dotOptions == CompactBoringNodes &&
297	        (isIntruderRule ru || isFreshRule ru) = do
298	            ps <- psM
299	            as <- asM
300	            cs <- csM
301	            let lbl | outgoingEdge = show v ++ " : " ++ showDotRuleCaseName ru
302	                    | otherwise       = concatMap snd as
303	            nid <- mkSimpleNode lbl []
304	            return [ (key, nid) | (key, _) <- ps ++ as ++ cs ]
305	      -- full record syntax
306	      | otherwise = do
307	            ps <- psM
308	            as <- asM
309	            cs <- csM
310	            fmap snd $ liftDot $ (`D.record` attrs) $
311	              D.vcat $ map D.hcat $ map (map (uncurry D.portField)) $
312	              filter (not . null) [ps, as, cs]
313	      where
314	        psM = do
315	          row <- mapM (\(i, p) -> do
316	            lbl <- renderLNFact p
317	            return (Just (Left i), lbl)
318	            ) $ enumPrems ru
319	          return $ renderRow row
320	        asM = do
321	          ruleLabel <- ruleLabelM
322	          return $ renderRow [ (Nothing, ruleLabel ) ]
323	        csM = do
324	          row <- mapM (\(i, c) -> do
325	            lbl <- renderLNFact c
326	            return (Just (Right i), lbl)
327	            ) $ enumConcs ru
328	          return $ renderRow row
329	
330	        ruleLabelM = do
331	          showAutoSource <- asks (get ((L..) goShowAutoSource gOptions) . fst3)
332	          lbl <- if showAutoSource

389	                    maybe False p (resolveNodeConcFact src graph)

412	-- | Dot a legend listing all abbreviations by adding a sink node with a suitable HTML table label.
413	generateLegend :: SeDot ()
414	generateLegend = do
415	  (graph, _, dotOptions) <- ask
416	  let abbrevs = get gAbbreviations graph
417	  -- Skip generating anything if no abbreviations exist.
418	  unless (null abbrevs) $ do
419	    nLegend <- liftDot $ D.scope (do
420	      D.attribute ("rank", "sink")
421	      let sortedAbbrevs = topoSortAbbrevs $ zip [0..] $
422	            sortOn (Data.Ord.Down . render . Sys.prettyLNTerm . fst) $ M.elems abbrevs
423	          labelColor = get doAbbrevColor dotOptions
424	          htmlLabel = D.htmlLabel $ abbrevLabel sortedAbbrevs labelColor
425	      D.node [("shape", "plain"), htmlLabel])
426	    -- We add invisible edges from all sink nodes of the graph to the legend node to place it somewhere in the middle of the bottom row.
427	    -- We only add edges from the sink nodes because edges from earlier nodes will be routed avoid later nodes (even if they are invisible) and create constraints that lead to excessive whitespace on the edges of the graph.
428	    let sinks = getGraphSinks graph
429	    dotIds <- getM dsNodes
430	    mapM_ (\nsink ->
431	      case M.lookup (get nNodeId nsink) dotIds of
432	      Nothing -> pure ()
433	      Just nid -> liftDot $ D.edge nid nLegend [("style", "invis")]) sinks
434	  where
435	    -- | Render all abbreviations as a table using graphviz' HTML notation.
436	    abbrevLabel sortedAbbrevs labelColor =
437	      let tableAttributes = [Border 1, CellBorder 0, CellSpacing 3, CellPadding 1] in
438	        Table $ HTable Nothing tableAttributes $ map (renderLine labelColor) sortedAbbrevs
439	
440	    -- | Render an abbreviation to a table row in the legend using graphviz' HTML notation.
441	    renderLine :: D.Color -> (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion) -> Row
442	    renderLine labelColor (abbrevName, recursiveExpansion) =
443	      let cellAttributes = [Align HLeft, VAlign HTop]
444	          font txt = D.Text [Font [Color labelColor] txt]
445	          name = LabelCell cellAttributes (font [Str $ T.pack $ render $ Sys.prettyLNTerm abbrevName])
446	          eq = LabelCell cellAttributes (D.Text [Str $ "="])
447	          expansion = render $ Sys.prettyLNTerm recursiveExpansion
448	          -- The expansions can get pretty big, i.e. span multiple lines. To handle linebreaks we replace them with HTML <br> tags.
449	          expansionBR = LabelCell cellAttributes (D.Text $ expansion `joinLinesWith` Newline [Align HLeft]) in
450	      Cells [name, eq, expansionBR]
451	
452	    -- | Replace newlines in `s` with the given separator.
453	    joinLinesWith :: String -> TextItem -> D.Text
454	    joinLinesWith s sep = intersperse sep $ map (Str . T.pack) $ lines s
455	
456	    -- | Do a topological sort of abbreviations to ensure that we print abbreviations in the order in which they are defined.
457	    -- We define a partial order where for two abbreviations A & B, it holds A < B if A appears in the recursive expansion of B.
458	    -- To extend the partial order to all abbreviations we do a topological sort on the graph where the nodes are abbreviations and edges are based on the partial order.
459	    topoSortAbbrevs :: [(Int, (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion))] -> [(AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion)]
460	    topoSortAbbrevs keyedElems =
461	      let edgeList = map (\(key1, node) ->
462	                            let outlist = findLegendEdges keyedElems node in
463	                            (node, key1, outlist)) keyedElems
464	          (graph, vf, _) = G.graphFromEdges edgeList
465	          vertices = G.topSort graph in
466	      map (\v -> fst3 $ vf v) vertices
467	
468	    -- | We create an edge A -> B between two abbreviations A & B if A appears in the recursive expansion of B.
469	    findLegendEdges :: [(Int, (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion))] -> (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion) -> [Int]
470	    findLegendEdges keyedElems (abbrevName1, _) =
471	      mapMaybe (\(key2, (_, recursiveExpansion2)) ->
472	                  if isProperSubterm abbrevName1 recursiveExpansion2
473	                  then Just key2
474	                  else Nothing) keyedElems

525	-- Simple hash function to amplify differences between names
526	simpleHash :: String -> Int
527	simpleHash = foldl (\ acc c -> acc * 31 + ord c) 7
528	
529	-- Function to generate a value based on the role name
530	generateValue :: String -> Double
531	generateValue s = fromIntegral (simpleHash s `mod` 360) / 360.0
532	
533	-- Function to generate a color based on the role name with more aesthetic colors
534	roleColor :: String -> String
535	roleColor name = printf "#%02X%02X%02X%02X" r g b alpha
536	  where
537	    v = generateValue name
538	    -- Adjust saturation and value to get more pleasant colors
539	    RGB rf gf bf = hsvToRGB (HSV (v * 360) 0.75 0.85) :: RGB Double  -- Higher saturation and brightness
540	    r = floor (rf * 255) :: Int
541	    g = floor (gf * 255) :: Int
542	    b = floor (bf * 255) :: Int
543	    alpha :: Int
544	    alpha = floor (255 * (0.3 :: Double))

548	dotCluster (Cluster name nodes _) = do
549	    let baseName = fromMaybe "Undefined" (extractBaseName name)
550	    let color = roleColor baseName
551	    roleCluster name $ do
552	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("nodesep", "0.6")
553	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("ranksep", "0.6")
554	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("label", name)
555	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("style", "filled") -- Set style to filled to apply fillcolor
556	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("color", color)
557	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("penwidth", "2")
558	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("fillcolor", color) -- Use roleColor to set the fillcolor
559	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("overlap", "false")
560	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("sep", "4")
561	
562	        mapM_ (\node -> dotNodeCompact node (Just color)) nodes
563	
564	-- | Compute proper colors for all less-edges.
565	-- Computing the colors requires all less-edges of the graph, so we cannot do it per edge.
566	-- This is why we split up the edges here and use two different functions to convert them to a dot.
567	mergeLessEdges :: [Edge] -> ([(NodeId, NodeId, String)], [Edge])
568	mergeLessEdges edges = (merged, rest)
569	  where
570	    rest = filter (not . isLessEdge) edges
571	    merged =
572	      let lessEdges = mapMaybe extractLessEdge edges in
573	      map getAllRToC $ eqClasses (\(LessAtom x y _) -> (x, y)) lessEdges
574	
575	    isLessEdge :: Edge -> Bool
576	    isLessEdge (LessEdge _) = True
577	    isLessEdge _ = False
578	
579	    extractLessEdge :: Edge -> Maybe LessAtom
580	    extractLessEdge (LessEdge e) = Just e
581	    extractLessEdge _ = Nothing
582	
583	    getAllRToC :: [LessAtom] -> (NodeId, NodeId, String)
584	    -- SAFETY: Output of eqClasses never contains the empty list.
585	    getAllRToC [] = error "empty list"
586	    getAllRToC xs@(x:_) =
587	      (get laSmaller x, get laLarger x,
588	        -- Sort order is reversed to put the "most important reason" first.
589	        allRtoColors (sortBy (comparing Data.Ord.Down) $ map (get laReason) xs))
590	
591	    allRtoColors :: [Reason] -> String
592	    allRtoColors reasons =
593	      let len = length reasons
594	          per = if len >1 then ";" ++ show ((1 :: Double)/fromIntegral len) else ""
595	          colors = map toColor reasons
596	          scaledColors = map (++per) colors
597	      in intercalate ":" scaledColors
598	
599	    toColor :: Reason -> String
600	    toColor r = case r of
601	         Adversary      -> "red"
602	         Formula        -> "black"
603	         Fresh          -> "blue3"
604	         InjectiveFacts -> "purple"
